question.text: A dealer who enters trades on the trading workstation of a trading member for Exchange-Traded Currency Derivatives must have which certification?

answer.text: NISM-Series-I: Currency Derivatives or NISM-Series-XIII Common Derivatives Certification Examination

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "a dealer (who will enter deal through TWS) of the trading member should successfully pass the NISM-Series-I: Currency Derivatives or NISM-Series-XIII Common Derivatives Certification Examination." **The Trap:** NISM-Series-VIII is for equity derivatives dealers — not currency derivatives. The currency-specific certification is Series-I (or the combined Series-XIII). **Cross-Exam Context:** This is the examination for which this very study material is the preparation guide. The NISM Series-I: Currency Derivatives certification is the mandatory qualification for currency derivatives dealers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Currency derivatives dealer = NISM Series-I or XIII. Equity derivatives dealer = NISM Series-VIII. Different product, different certification requirement.
